requirement_activation_slices.active_scope: Reject secret-bearing continuation content and explicit prohibited authentication tags in the canonical boundary.
requirement_activation_slices.remaining_scope: Verify every public host and renderer refuses authentication material through the activated task-input capability.
requirement_activation_slices.evidence: Canonical codecs and tagged-field security checks are active while public host capability paths remain dormant.

requirement_activation_slices.active_scope: Enforce bounded typed request aggregates and complete provider-free request, resolution, and continuation projection.
requirement_activation_slices.remaining_scope: Advertise, await, and return the request through capable and incapable production model hosts.
requirement_activation_slices.evidence: The provider-free lifecycle is executable and typed without activating any model capability registry.

requirement_activation_slices.active_scope: Validate the bounded related-question aggregate and reject malformed selection collections and references.
requirement_activation_slices.remaining_scope: Verify production model hosts render and return one related question bundle through the reserved capability.
requirement_activation_slices.evidence: Canonical aggregate and selection invariants are active while host rendering remains planned.

requirement_activation_slices.active_scope: Reject empty, mutable, duplicate, untyped, or more-than-three-question canonical request aggregates.
requirement_activation_slices.remaining_scope: Verify production model capability parsing rejects more than three requested questions before side effects.
requirement_activation_slices.evidence: The canonical constructor owns the three-question limit; provider batch enforcement remains planned.

requirement_activation_slices.active_scope: Reject provider secrets, revision drift, invalid JSON, and unsafe continuation snapshot content before restoration.
requirement_activation_slices.remaining_scope: Verify activated provider call handling never executes actions, reads process input, assumes a renderer, or fabricates human provenance.
requirement_activation_slices.evidence: Continuation security is active at the canonical codec boundary while provider execution policy remains planned.

requirement_activation_slices.active_scope: Reject secret-bearing snapshots and explicit password, key, token, payment, and authentication-challenge tags.
requirement_activation_slices.remaining_scope: Verify every activated CLI, SDK, HTTP, MCP, A2A, task, and nested host refuses secret collection end to end.
requirement_activation_slices.evidence: Canonical tagged-field and snapshot rejection is active while production host surfaces remain unadvertised.

requirement_activation_slices.active_owner: core stream and task observer boundary
requirement_activation_slices.active_scope: Project opaque correlation through content-safe observer tokens and fail closed on forged content or invalid lifecycle state.
requirement_activation_slices.remaining_scope: Verify complete telemetry retention, wait timing, category, duplicate, stale, cancellation, expiry, and resume behavior across every public surface.
requirement_activation_slices.evidence: Core observer projection and security are active; the comprehensive cross-surface telemetry contract remains planned.

replacements.evidence: Canonical types exist while all production capability registries remain unadvertised.
